https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/commit/.commit_message: [Performance] Refactor heading and code syntax highlighting (#6046)

description.text:
## Description

Okay, I think it's time to make this PR a tad more visible. **~~This PR is not yet ready for review!~~ It's ready now!** You may, however, if you're curious, build it and run it in test mode (`yarn test-gui --clean`) to have a first look.

I must admit, I got really pissed with tons of pieces of the codebase and decided to clean up even completely unrelated parts (such as the FSAL). That explains the hefty amount of changed files (188 at the time of writing).

Was this a clever decision?

No. Not at all.

Was it a satisfying action?

You bet!

## Changes

The following ist is guaranteed to be incomplete, but I'll give it a shot:

1. Exchanged Codemirror 5 with Codemirror 6 (currently: Only the main editor, still todo: the smaller code editors)
2. While migrating the plugins from 5->6 gave most of them a facelift, thanks to, first, a MUCH better API, and second, a godsent AST
3. **Documents are now managed CENTRALLY in the DocumentsProvider**. Why does that matter? Because now there is **one** source of truth for all documents. This makes the shutdown logic much simpler, keeps every editor leaf in sync, and a ton of other benefits. Plus, it's fancy!
4. I finally decided to merge the Meta-descriptors in the FSAL with the "regular" ones. The distinction into meta and non-meta descriptors didn't make sense and is 100% on me. Now it's much better.
5. Disentangled the FSAL, Documents, and Target providers which previously were much tighter entangled. This had the problem of a thousand and one hard-to-track side-effects between the providers.
6. The FSAL now specifically has two utility functions that will return a descriptor for a random path on disk (as long as it's supported) and one that will return a (supported) file's contents. No more poking around in the guts of a provider.

## Additional information

Still TODO:

- [x] Move the CodeEditor to Codemirror 6
- [x] Re-implement the TableEditor
- [x] Allow for granular control of the renderers (currently hardcoded due to limitations in my imagination regarding the new config system)
- [x] Put the extensions into compartments for easy-ish reconfiguration of existing documents
- [x] Re-enable spell-checking
- [x] Implement a Markdown linter since that re-uses 99% of the code required for the new spell checker and because I can.
- [x] Write a few code-fold extensions to allow for better code folding, even where we're limited to the older `StreamParser`s
- [x] Better styling for the tooltips (which are now no longer sponsored by tippy but by the library itself)
- [x] Better styling for the autocompletes (strictly speaking also "only" tooltips)
- [x] Re-implement the emphasis-renderer because now we can do that properly (thanks to the AST)
- [x] More careful (secure) inline-HTML rendering (since we can do that now, thanks to the AST)
- [x] Re-implement iFrame rendering
- [x] When the user closes the last tab, empty out the editor
- [x] Add an exemption for the autocorrect to not replace --- with an em dash when it's the first line of the file (collides with YAML frontmatter)
- [x] Spellchecker currently takes sometimes more than a minute to check a word (due to the overhead of approx. 50-70ms per IPC call), I need to bundle multiple words together (basically: string array in, array of booleans out)
- [x] Implement `==.+==`-highlighting
- [x] Fix styles of emphasis/strong emphasis in and outside of lists

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: macOS 12.6 (Silicon/ARM)

  longer.

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR reconfigures the renderer handling so that changing the rendering mode from the statusbar immediately updates the editor.

Closes #6007

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
Functionally, the `renderingModeToggle` was updated to use `dispatch` events to update the config rather than `window.config.set`, and an empty dispatch was added so that the changed mode is applied without further interaction from the user.

Additionally, the code was refactored for maintainability, notably moving the configuration of renderers to its own function.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 26

If I click on the text, the rendering is applied. ### Zettlr version Zettlr 4.0.0-beta.2 ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method (Other) deb package ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [ ] macOS - [x] Linux ### Architecture - [x] Intel 64bit - [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon) ### Operating System Version Ubuntu 22.04 ### Additional Information _No response_

  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR refactors the code-background plugin to only draw the layer for the visible ranges. The code was also simplified.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
The layers are only drawn for visible ranges rather than the whole document to improve performance.

The node test logic was simplified. For the codeblocks, we check for either `CodeText` or `CommentBlock` because code blocks, fenced code, and yaml blocks all keep content within `CodeText` nodes. This allows us to simplify the range calculation, as well.

The `try...catch` blocks were removed as I believe they are no longer necessary since [this commit](https://github.com/codemirror/view/commit/7235126c19c9f285a94a79dd79c3502ae2086be6) in `codemirror/view` 

The `code` class is no longer assigned using the layer. I do not think this is necessary, as the `code` class is assigned by the other code syntax extension.

Commented out code and outdated comments were removed.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 26

<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

     files for examples)

     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR implements a debounced word counting plugin to improve typing performance.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
The word count computation was moved to a `ViewPlugin` so that it can be debounced using `setTimeout()`. It uses a `StateEffect` to update the existing `countField` value.

Event listeners were added for the new `updateWordCountEffect` so that the UI shows updated counts when they arrive rather than waiting for cursor activity.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->
Currently, word counts are recomputed every keypress. In larger documents (>50,000 words) this starts to add a small but noticeable, and increasing, delay. By debouncing the counting, typing performance is dramatically improved in large documents. 

Word counts are no longer live, however, they are updated the moment the transaction is dispatched.

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR restores pilcrows on blank lines

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
My previous PR cleaning up the pilcrow handling caused a regression where pilcrows were not shown on blank lines. This refactor is a more straigtforward approach and still ignores drawing pilcrows within code blocks.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> Fedora 43

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->

This PR provides a wholly rewritten table editor. The aim is to fix several data loss issues that are scattered around the repository right now. For that, the table editor now hooks much more into the novel CodeMirror 6 API and ensures that any changes -- inside and outside of tables -- is always retained in the main editor, and never in some additional state that needs to be synchronized. This addresses the main issue that many people had where it was intransparent that they had to perform a special action before changes were persisted to the actual document.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->

* Completely remove the old TableEditor
* Provide a rewritten one that keeps all changes in the main view, and only provides a GUI on top of Markdown tables

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

Currently, this PR is in draft status, since I haven't been able to fully replicate the old editor's functionality yet.

Fixes the following issues:

* Fixes #5190
* Fixes #5102
* Fixes #5080
* Fixes #4657
* Fixes #3596
* Fixes #3519
* Fixes #3132
* Fixes #1999

### Available keyboard commands

Most actions that can be triggered via keyboard shortcuts. Even the table handlers actually just programmatically call these commands, because the CodeMirror API makes it really easy for us to compose even complex actions just with commands. An added benefit is that regardless of whether the user wants the table editor or not, they can properly work with tables. TableEditor users have a nice layout, while "raw" users still benefit from the new added commands.

IMPORTANT: These keyboard shortcuts currently are suggestions. I tried to be reasonable and ensure we don't have too many conflicts, but I expect some to change before merging this PR. Especially since the Zettlr keymap is getting pretty huge.

Here's the current, temporary keymap for table commands (note: `Mod` refers to `Cmd` on macOS, and `Ctrl` else):

* <kbd>Tab</kbd>: Move to the next cell (<kbd>Shift</kbd> for previous cell)
* <kbd>Enter</kbd>: Move to the next row (<kbd>Shift</kbd> for previous row)
* <kbd>Alt-Shift-ArrowUp</kbd> (macOS: <kbd>Ctrl-Shift-ArrowUp</kbd>): Add a row before the one where the cursor is in
* <kbd>Alt-ArrowUp</kbd> (macOS: <kbd>Ctrl-ArrowUp</kbd>): Swap the current row with the previous one (accounting for delimiting rows where necessary)
* <kbd>Alt-Shift-ArrowDown</kbd>  (macOS: <kbd>Ctrl-Shift-ArrowDown</kbd>): Add a row after the one where the cursor is in
* <kbd>Alt-ArrowDown</kbd>  (macOS: <kbd>Ctrl-ArrowDown</kbd>): Swap the current row with the next one (accounting for delimiting rows where necessary)
* <kbd>Alt-Shift-ArrowRight</kbd>  (macOS: <kbd>Ctrl-Shift-ArrowRight</kbd>): Add a new column to the right of the one where the cursor currently is in
* <kbd>Alt-ArrowRight</kbd>  (macOS: <kbd>Ctrl-ArrowRight</kbd>): Swap the current column with the next one
* <kbd>Alt-Shift-ArrowLeft</kbd>  (macOS: <kbd>Ctrl-Shift-ArrowLeft</kbd>): Add a new column to the left of the one where the cursor currently is in
* <kbd>Alt-ArrowLeft</kbd>  (macOS: <kbd>Ctrl-ArrowLeft</kbd>): Swap the current column with the previous one
* <kbd>Shift-Mod-k</kbd>: Delete the current column
* <kbd>Mod-Backspace</kbd>: Clears out the current column (i.e., inserts whitespace)
* <kbd>Shift-Mod-Backspace</kbd>: Clears out the current row (i.e., inserts whitespace)
* <kbd>Alt-Shift-Mod-Backspace</kbd>: Clears out the entire table
* <kbd>Ctrl-c</kbd>: Aligns the current column center (by placing colons in the delim row)
* <kbd>Ctrl-l</kbd>: Aligns the current column left
* <kbd>Ctrl-r</kbd>: Aligns the current column right
* <kbd>Mod-Shift-a</kbd>: Aligns the table at the main selection head (not left/right/center, but adds the appropriate padding so that it is formatted nicely. Requires a monospaced font for proper visuals.)

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: macOS Sonoma 14.5

closed_issues.summary: Moving the mouse toward the top left corner of the app causes Table Alignment hover menu to shows up.

description.text:
### Description

When a note having a markdown file containing a table is opened and the Table Editor option enabled, hovering the mouse to the left of the "File manager" button (or to the left of "File button" with Native Window Appearance disabled) on the toolbar will cause the Table Alignment hover menu to appear when it should not.

### Reproducing

1. Open Preference > Editor and make sure Table Editor is enabled
2. Create an empty Markdown file
3. Create a Table

If Native Windows Appearance option is enabled, hovering the mouse to the left of "File manager" button on the toolbar will cause the Table Alignment hover menu to show up.

If Native Windows Appearance option is disabled, hovering the mouse to the left of "File" button on the toolbar will cause the Table Alignment hover menu to show up.


### Zettlr Version

Stable (most recent version)

### Specify version

3.1.1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [X] Linux

### Architecture

- [X] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit

### Operating System Version

Fedora 39 (Xfce)

### Additional Information

It created many instances of all table hover items when the bug happen.

![image](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/assets/130038743/dc38c089-ccfc-481f-9f95-97c03e7b3368)

closed_issues.summary: Data disappear from table when saving

description.text:
### Description

Hello, 

Sorry if this has already been discussed, but I didn't find any issue like this one.

When I save a file with a table (at least 2 rows and 2 columns), data disappear from the last edited cell, or sometimes it switches back to the previous version.

It seems kind of random, and I haven't noticed any clue on why this is happening.

Also, if I use CTRL+Z, nothing happens. Worst, it cancels the last edit I made before adding data to the table.
For example, if I edited a title then several cells in the table, using CRTL+Z will only affect the edition made in the title.

My configuration:
- Zettlr 3.0.5 (Windows)
- Theme: Berlin with dark mode
- "Accentuation du rendu" activated (sorry, I don't know how this option is called in English…)

It is very annoying since it makes the use of table unstable and unreliable.

Is this a documented issue?

Regards,

### Reproducing

As far as I know, you only need to create a table with at least 2 rows and 2 columns and add some data.
Then, save your file.

### Zettlr Version

Stable (most recent version)

### Specify version

3.0.5

### Installation Method

None

### Your Platform

- [X] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit

### Operating System Version

Windows 10

### Additional Information

_No response_

description.text:
### Description

I'm writing my thesis, it's therefore a long document (right now around 46k characters) and wanted to introduce a table. After reading the table editor instructions after a few failed tries (sorry, not a very intuitive feature), I created a table of around 8 rows and 4 columns. As soon as it was saved, and I wanted to start typing again, the typing was EXTREMELY slow and also did not type correctly - as it was catching every other typed letter.

### Reproducing

Create a long document (mine is around 45k characters) with many in-text citations. Create a large table and start writing. Not sure if reproducible or just me.

closed_issues.summary: Large tables cause the view to scroll down upon update

description.text:
### Description
Adding or removing rows or columns to a table can cause the view to scroll down the page.
Changing the theme produces the same effect.

My guess is that the table momentarily becomes raw text, which is shorter than a table row.
Then, the text expands back into a table and pushes the screen down with it

### Reproducing
Create a file with a (sufficiently large) table at the top and many lines of text at the bottom.
Add a row to the bottom of the table.

**or**: while the large table is in frame, change themes.

### Zettlr Version
2.3.0 (UUID: 56bb8ed2-45e8-4c39-a961-66a2589fd908)
running on win32 10.0.19043 (architecture: x64)

ranked_scenarios.summary: feat: Make link rendering native/improve links

description.text:
## Description

This PR changes how the link renderer works. This entails changes in the Link and Image parser that seem a bit janky, but that is (as far as I could tell) as it is supposed to be. With this PR, Link Rendering does not render the Link as an actual `<a>`-element, which enables a more native experience. The rendering works instead like the emphasis renderer by simply hiding elements that are not visible to the user on export. This also has effects on clicking and the tooltips with link previews, since that is now also native instead of working via hooks into the various events on the custom anchor elements and the tippy tooltips which, while looking cool, differ from CodeMirror's native Tooltips.

## Changes

* The "sloppy parser" now properly parses inline syntax in the ALT text elements of Markdown links and images, making the Editor CSS render inline elements properly
* A few changes to the CSS files since I was somehow overwriting a lot of the default styles in unnecessary ways
* Updates to the Hyperlink-tooltip plugin to make it more resilient to the various inline elements that can occur in Markdown links; it now reliably detects a link and extracts the proper URL to generate the preview
* The same updates were applied to the DOM event handlers that ensure that Cmd/Ctrl clicking anywhere within a Markdown link will open the proper link
* A few improvements to how the link previews are rendered

## Additional information

Working with the Lezer LR parse trees can be a pain, and one issue I have stumbled upon is that **I cannot for the love of god get the Markdown AST parser to properly construct inline HTML elements within the ALT text description of Markdown links or images, which means that if you use the built-in Markdown-to-HTML converter ("Copy as HTML" as well as various HTML previews of files around the app), it is incapable of properly rendering inline elements as of now and will only show the plain text.** Please note that this limitation is not new; the previous link parser treated everything just as plain text; now the link parser at least parses inline formatting; even though the Markdown AST parser still doesn't. So we're slowly getting there, I guess.

The regular Pandoc exports are **not affected** by this limitation.

If someone sees what I'm doing wrong, please come forward and suggest a fix!

***

Fixes #4684
Fixed #4592
Fixes #4024

***

Tested on: macOS 14.0 Sonoma w/ MacBook Pro 14in M2 Pro

closed_issues.summary: Middle Clicking Links opens Websites in Electron Browser instead of the System's Webbrowser

description.text:
### Description

Title says it.

Whenever I middle click (pressing down the mouse wheel) on a Link to a Website Zettlr opens the Site inside a new Window with Electron.

This _might_ also be a **security problem** since opening rogue Websites in said Electron window might make you vulnerable to attacks when it is not set up for this in the first place, but I don't really know, so I leave this for others to judge.

### Reproducing

- Open a markdown file with Zettlr
- Paste `[Test Link](https://www.example.com)` into the file
- Middle Click the Link

### Zettlr Version

Stable (most recent version)

### Specify version

3.0.1

### Installation Method

AUR (Arch Repository)

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [X] Linux

### Architecture

- [X] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit

### Operating System Version

Arch Linux

### Additional Information

I use the Version from the official Arch repository, no further modifications done.

I posted this on Reddit and was advised to open an issue about this:
https://www.reddit.com/r/Zettlr/comments/16nuy29/middle_clicking_links_opens_built_in_browser/

### Description

More testing. :)

In Zettlr `2.*` I was able to render emphasis/italics inside a markdown link.
For example, `[André Comte Sponville, _Diccionario filosófico_](...)` would be rendered [André Comte Sponville, _Diccionario filosófico_](...) (see italics).

This no longer seems to be true in Zettlr `3.0`.

closed_issues.summary: Link text inside strikethrough region does not get styled

description.text:
### Description

I would expect strikethrough styling to be applied to the text of a link inside of it, but it's not.  (Note that this _does_ work for _bold_ text.)

### Reproducing

Include this text snippet in a document:

```
**bold text with [a link](https://google.com) inside**

~~strikethrough text with [a link](https://github.com) inside~~

[a link with ~~strikethrough text~~ inside](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/new?assignees=&labels=bug&template=bug_report.yml)
```

Observe that the link text in the bold section is bolded, but the link text in the strikethrough section is not struck through.

This is not because links cannot be struck through: the third example shows that styling _inside_ of the link text is applied correctly.

### Zettlr Version

Stable (most recent version)

### Specify version

2.3.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub
